content:
💡 episteme | Kotlin
🎯 Primary Use Case: A versatile, privacy-conscious offline document and e-book reader for Android devices.
✨ Key Features: • Supports PDF, EPUB, MOBI, AZW3, Markdown, HTML, and plain text. • Ink and text annotations for PDFs. • Native parsing for MOBI/AZW3 and EPUB. • Customizable e-book reading (font size, line spacing, margins, custom fonts). • Text-to-Speech (TTS) functionality.
📖 Summary: Episteme Reader is a native Android application for reading various document and e-book formats offline. It focuses on privacy and offers features like annotations, text-to-speech, and customizable reading experiences.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ claude-swarm | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Orchestrating and managing parallel, long-running autonomous coding sessions using Claude Code workers, with robust state persistence, behavioral governance, and real-time monitoring.
✨ Key Features: • Persistent State Management • Parallel Claude Code Workers (tmux) • Isolated Git Worktree Execution • Protocol-Based Behavioral Governance • Real-time Web Dashboard
📖 Summary: Claude Swarm is a TypeScript-based MCP server designed to orchestrate parallel Claude Code worker swarms for long-running autonomous coding sessions. It features persistent state management, parallel worker execution using tmux and isolated git worktrees, and protocol-based behavioral governance to enforce constraints during runtime.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 scantailor-advanced | C++
🎯 Primary Use Case: Post-processing scanned documents to prepare them for digital assembly into formats like PDF or DjVu.
✨ Key Features: • Page splitting • Deskewing • Adding/removing borders • Content selection • Auto margins
📖 Summary: ScanTailor Advanced is an interactive post-processing tool for scanned pages, merging and enhancing features from previous versions. It automates tasks like page splitting, deskewing, border adjustment, and content selection, preparing raw scans for digital assembly into formats like PDF or DjVu.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 openlibrary | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To build and maintain a comprehensive, community-driven catalog of all books ever published, making information about them accessible online.
✨ Key Features: • Open, editable library catalog • Web page for every book ever published • Community contributions • Developer documentation and quick start guides
📖 Summary: Open Library is an open-source project aiming to create a web page for every book ever published. It functions as an editable library catalog, allowing for community contributions to build a comprehensive database of literary works.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 pathway | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Building and deploying real-time data processing pipelines, analytics, and LLM applications using a unified Python API.
✨ Key Features: • Python ETL framework • Stream processing • Real-time analytics • LLM pipelines and RAG
📖 Summary: Pathway is a Python ETL framework designed for efficient stream processing, real-time analytics, and building LLM pipelines, including RAG. It offers a flexible Python API that works seamlessly with ML libraries, supporting both batch and streaming data for development and production. Its core is a scalable Rust engine that enables incremental computation, multithreading, multiprocessing, and distributed processing, with easy deployment options via Docker and Kubernetes.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
